what: FlyDSL stage2 t16x128x128_atomic for bs=128/E=257/d=256 with ksplit=2 cktile_moe stage1
why: bs=512/E=257 improved with FlyDSL stage2 (v143). Try same for bs=128/E=257 which currently uses default cktile_moe stage2. FlyDSL atomic stage2 with tile_m=16 was best for similar shapes.

what: CK 2-stage block_m=32 + FlyDSL stage2 t16x128x128 for bs=128/E=257/d=256
why: v148 crashed with block_m=16 due to quant kernel needing block_m%32==0. Use block_m=32 instead. CK stage1 + FlyDSL stage2 improved bs=512/E=257 in v143/v144.

what: 4-WG stage1 + FlyDSL stage2 for bs=128/E=33/d=512 with block_m=128
why: bs=512/E=33/d=512 improved significantly with 4-WG M128 + FlyDSL (v138). bs=128 with ~4.5 tokens/expert could also benefit from the same approach vs cktile_moe ksplit=2 block_m=64.

bs=128/E=257 at 176us BM is weakest shape relative to reference. With d=256, K=7168 is large relative to expert dim. ksplit=4 gives more parallelism over K dimension. ksplit=2 works well for bs=16/E=257, maybe ksplit=4 better for bs=128.
